html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 牧牛于野 - Literally 'herding cattle in the wild,' referring to a person tending cattle in an open field.
  • 数亡其牛 - Means 'frequently losing his cattle,' indicating repeated incidents of cattle going missing.
  • 异而掘之 - Means 'finding it strange, he dug it up,' describing the action taken upon noticing the unusual condition.
  • 得琮形如今制 - Means 'found a ritual object shaped like today's,' indicating that the object was similar to modern ones but made of white jade.
  • 发之 - Means 'opened it,' describing the action of opening the tomb.
  • 验款识 - Means 'verified the inscriptions,' indicating that the inscriptions were checked to confirm their authenticity.
  • 皆宽袖 - Means 'all had wide sleeves,' describing the style of the clothing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宋 - Refers to the Song dynasty, a period of significant cultural and technological development in Chinese history.
  • 郭彖 - The author of the text, a scholar and official during the Song dynasty.
  • 宣政间 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Huizong of Song, known for its cultural and artistic achievements.
  • 开元 - Refers to the Kaiyuan era of the Tang dynasty (713–741), a period of prosperity and cultural flourishing.
  • 祭地黄琮 - A ritual object used in the Tang dynasty for earth worship, symbolizing the connection between the earthly realm and the divine.
  • 建炎 - Refers to the early years of the Jianyan era (1127–1130), a period marked by the Jurchen invasion and the fall of the Northern Song dynasty.
  • 寇乱 - Refers to the Jurchen invasion, which led to the fall of the Northern Song dynasty and the establishment of the Southern Song dynasty.
  • 三代 - Refers to the Shang, Zhou, and Qin dynasties, a period of significant cultural and political development in Chinese history.
  • 古铜钟鼎 - Ancient bronze bells and tripods, which were important ritual objects in Chinese culture, often used in religious ceremonies and as symbols of power and status.

Proper Nouns

  • 郭彖 - The author of the text.
  • 长安 - The ancient capital city of the Han and Tang dynasties, now known as Xi'an.
  • 开元 - The Kaiyuan era of the Tang dynasty (713–741).
  • 祭地黄琮 - A ritual object used in the Tang dynasty for earth worship.
  • 建炎 - The early years of the Jianyan era (1127–1130).
  • 寇乱 - The Jurchen invasion and the fall of the Northern Song dynasty.
  • 三代 - The Shang, Zhou, and Qin dynasties.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 希世寵幸 - Means 'to seek the favor of the world' or 'to gain the emperor's favor.'
  • 數召入禁中 - Means 'often summoned into the inner palace.'
  • 再拜殿下 - A formal greeting, meaning 'to bow twice before the throne.'
  • 長揖而坐 - Means 'to bow deeply and then sit down.'
  • 罔上之诛 - Means 'punishment for deceiving the emperor.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 林靈素 - A real historical figure, a Taoist magician who gained significant influence during the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 九華安妃 - A Taoist deity or a figure of high rank in the Taoist pantheon.
  • 玉清上真 - Refers to a high-ranking Taoist deity.
  • 神霄某夫人 - Another Taoist deity or a figure of high rank in the Taoist pantheon.
  • 露台妓李師師 - A famous courtesan of the time, known for her beauty and talent.
  • 御爐火箸 - A tool used in the imperial court, often for ceremonial purposes.
  • 內侍 - Refers to eunuchs or court attendants.
  • 無孤尾者 - A metaphorical expression, possibly referring to a lack of a tail, which could symbolize something incomplete or flawed.

Proper Nouns

  • 宣和 - The era name of Emperor Huizong of Song.
  • 韓亞卿 - The author or recorder of the story.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 左贲字彦文 - Zuo Ben, courtesy name Yanwen, where '字' (courtesy name) is a traditional Chinese practice of giving a name for formal or social use.
  • 有道术 - Possessed of Daoist arts, referring to mystical or magical abilities.
  • 游京师依段氏 - Travelled to the capital and relied on the Duan family, indicating a period of residence and patronage.
  • 甚礼重之 - Treated him with great respect, emphasizing the high regard in which he was held.
  • 拜章祈福 - Submitted a petition for blessings, a ritual act to seek divine favor.
  • 乙夜羽衣伏坛上 - At the second watch, he was dressed in a feathered robe and stood on the altar, describing a mystical or ritual appearance.
  • 五鼓姑苏 - At the fifth watch, he was in Gusu, a place name and time reference, possibly indicating a journey or transformation.
  • 怆然不怿久之 - He was deeply saddened and unhappy for a long time, expressing a prolonged emotional state.
  • 禄筭尚永 - His fate is still long, suggesting that his life will continue for a considerable period.
  • 适出金阙 - Just emerged from the Golden Gate, a metaphorical reference to a divine or celestial realm.
  • 力见邀 - Was strongly invited, indicating a compelling summons.
  • 已不可辞 - Could not refuse, emphasizing the inevitability of the invitation.
  • 本意且欲住世广行利益 - Originally intended to stay in the world and widely perform good deeds, expressing his initial purpose.
  • 志不遂 - His wishes were not fulfilled, indicating unmet aspirations.
  • 具棺衾敛之 - Prepared a coffin and shroud for him, a formal act of preparation for burial.
  • 舁棺送之 - Carried the coffin to send him, describing the act of transporting the body.
  • 辞不肯往 - Refused to go, indicating reluctance or refusal.
  • 棺必无尸 - The coffin must be empty, suggesting a belief in the absence of a physical body.
  • 业此久矣 - Have been doing this for a long time, indicating long-term experience.
  • 凡人之肥瘠大小 - The fatness, thinness, size, and smallness of ordinary people, describing physical characteristics.
  • 举棺即知之 - Can know by lifting the coffin, indicating a method of determining the contents.
  • 此其轻 - This one is light, suggesting a lighter weight.
  • 假致它物 - Must be a substitute for something else, indicating a belief in a non-physical presence.
  • 至彼或遭讯诘 - When they get there, they might be questioned, suggesting potential consequences.
  • 苟为累，吾自当之 - If it is a burden, I will take it upon myself, indicating a willingness to bear responsibility.
  • 发视 - Opened to see, describing the act of inspecting the contents.
  • 衣衾而已 - Only clothing and shroud, indicating the absence of a physical body.
  • 尸解 - Transformation of the body, a Daoist concept of spiritual transformation without physical death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 左贲 - A historical figure known for his Daoist practices and mystical abilities.
  • 段氏 - A noble family in the capital, known for their patronage of scholars and mystics.
  • 金阙 - A metaphorical reference to a divine or celestial realm, often associated with Daoist deities.
  • 姑苏 - A place name, possibly referring to Suzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 尸解 - A Daoist concept of spiritual transformation, where the body is left behind, and the soul ascends to heaven.

Proper Nouns

  • 左贲 - A historical figure.
  • 段氏 - A noble family.
  • 金阙 - A metaphorical reference to a divine or celestial realm.
  • 姑苏 - A place name, possibly referring to Suzhou.
  • 尸解 - A Daoist concept of spiritual transformation.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 通判 - Local administrative official in the Song Dynasty, responsible for overseeing the prefecture and assisting the governor.
  • 丞郡青社 - Indicates that the official served as a deputy governor in Qingzhou (青州), which was part of the administrative division known as '青社.'
  • 秩满还里 - Refers to completing one's term of office and returning home.
  • 素慕神仙长生之说 - Literally means 'has always admired the teachings of immortals and longevity,' referring to the pursuit of Daoist practices for eternal life.
  • 故絮蓝缕 - Describes the appearance of the hermit, meaning 'worn-out clothes' or 'ragged attire.'
  • 疥癞狼藉 - Refers to the hermit's skin condition, meaning 'covered in sores and scabs.'
  • 顷在青州印施<度人经> - Means 'recently in Qingzhou, I printed and distributed the *Duren Jing* (The Scripture of Deliverance),' referring to a Daoist scripture.
  • 曩所施也 - Means 'what was given in the past,' confirming that the document is indeed the one the official had previously distributed.
  • 烧汞为白金 - Refers to the hermit's ability to transform mercury into white gold, a process often associated with alchemy.
  • 某不愿也 - A humble way of saying 'I do not want it,' where '某' is a modest self-reference.
  • 姑试一观 - Means 'just for a look,' indicating a temporary or experimental action.
  • 腰间取镪数百 - Refers to the hermit taking out several hundred pieces of silver from his waist.
  • 炽炭环之 - Means 'surrounding it with burning charcoal,' describing the alchemical process.
  • 解带间剂投其中 - Means 'adding a powder from his belt into it,' referring to the catalyst used in the transformation.
  • 真白金也 - Means 'it is truly white gold,' confirming the success of the alchemical process.
  • 佳茗 - Refers to fine tea, a common gift in Chinese culture.
  • 建茶 - Refers to tea from Jianzhou (建州), a region known for its high-quality tea.
  • 裹以坏布 - Means 'wrapped in tattered cloth,' describing the poor condition of the tea.
  • 虮虱仆缘 - Refers to lice and fleas crawling on the tea, emphasizing its unsanitary state.
  • 从者 - Means 'attendant' or 'servant,' referring to the official's personal servant.
  • 茶具 - Refers to tea utensils, necessary for preparing and drinking tea.
  • 顾炉中银铛取水煮之 - Means 'taking water from the silver pot on the stove and boiling it,' describing the preparation of the tea.
  • 揖孟举啜 - Means 'bowing and inviting him to drink,' a polite gesture.
  • 太热 - Means 'too hot,' indicating that the tea was not yet ready to drink.
  • 留候再温饮之 - Means 'leave it to be warmed again before drinking,' suggesting that the hermit will warm the tea later.
  • 果相恶耶 - Means 'do you really dislike me?' expressing disappointment or frustration.
  • 覆之 - Means 'covering it,' referring to the hermit covering the tea.
  • 不揖而起 - Means 'without bowing and standing up,' indicating the hermit's departure.
  • 送之门 - Means 'seeing him off at the door,' showing respect and politeness.
  • 所覆茗地皆黄金 - Means 'the place where the tea was covered turned into gold,' indicating the miraculous transformation.
  • 盏及铛茗所渍处表里皆金 - Means 'the cup and pot, where the tea had been, turned into gold inside and out,' further emphasizing the miraculous nature of the event.
  • 异人 - Refers to a person with extraordinary abilities or a sage.
  • 亟追访之 - Means 'hurriedly searching for him,' indicating the official's eagerness to find the hermit again.
  • 已失所在 - Means 'already lost his whereabouts,' indicating that the hermit had disappeared.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 通判 - A local administrative official in the Song Dynasty, responsible for overseeing the prefecture and assisting the governor.
  • 密州 - Refers to Muzhou, a region in present-day Shandong Province.
  • 青州 - Refers to Qingzhou, another region in present-day Shandong Province.
  • 神仙长生之说 - Refers to the Daoist belief in immortality and the pursuit of longevity through spiritual practices.
  • 度人经 - Refers to the *Duren Jing* (The Scripture of Deliverance), a significant Daoist scripture.
  • 烧汞为白金 - Refers to the alchemical process of transforming mercury into white gold, a practice often associated with Daoist alchemy.
  • 建茶 - Refers to tea from Jianzhou, a region known for its high-quality tea.
  • 银铛 - Refers to a silver pot used for boiling water, a common tea utensil in Chinese culture.
  • 异人 - Refers to a person with extraordinary abilities or a sage, often associated with Daoist or Buddhist traditions.

Proper Nouns

  • 通判 - A local administrative official in the Song Dynasty.
  • 密州 - Muzhou, a region in present-day Shandong Province.
  • 青州 - Qingzhou, another region in present-day Shandong Province.
  • 度人经 - The *Duren Jing* (The Scripture of Deliverance), a significant Daoist scripture.
  • 建茶 - Tea from Jianzhou, a region known for its high-quality tea.
  • 银铛 - A silver pot used for boiling water, a common tea utensil in Chinese culture.
  • 异人 - A person with extraordinary abilities or a sage.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 行脚 - A Buddhist term meaning 'to travel on foot for spiritual practice or study.'
  • 古废兰若 - Refers to an ancient abandoned temple or hermitage.
  • 墙屋颓圮 - Describes walls and buildings in a state of decay.
  • 土榻地炉 - Refers to a simple bed and a ground-level stove.
  • 瓦缶 - A clay pot or jar.
  • 煮芋 - Boiling or cooking yams.
  • 淳化中故绫纸度牒 - A piece of ancient silk paper from the Chuncheng era (990-994) used as a Buddhist ordination certificate.
  • 山蓣 - Another term for yams or Chinese yams.
  • 盐醯之属 - Refers to salt and vinegar, or other seasonings.
  • 荷插 - Carrying a hoe or farming tool.
  • 被发 - Having long hair hanging loose, often associated with a wild or unrefined appearance.
  • 黄毛 - Yellow hair, likely referring to a person with a wild or untamed appearance.
  • 败僧衲 - Tattered Buddhist robes.
  • 直入坐土榻 - Entering directly and sitting on the earthen bed.
  • 倚墙壁坐睡 - Leaning against a wall and sitting to sleep.
  • 失其人所在 - Disappearing or vanishing without a trace.
  • 瓦缶 - A clay pot or jar.
  • 四瓦缶 - Four clay pots.
  • 其一已空 - One of them was already empty.
  • 盖其人食之而出 - It seems that the person had eaten from it and left.
  • 余三缶皆芋粟 - The remaining three pots were filled with yams and millet.
  • 煮已麋热 - Cooked until soft and hot.
  • 若以饷客者 - As if they were meant to serve the guests.
  • 行岩谷荆莽中 - Walking through rocky valleys and dense undergrowth.
  • 得路还 - Finding the path and returning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 绍兴间 - Refers to the Shaoxing era (1131-1162) during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 湖南 - Refers to Hunan province, a region in southern China.
  • 淳化中 - Refers to the Chuncheng era (990-994) during the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 度牒 - A Buddhist ordination certificate, often made of silk paper.
  • 山蓣 - A type of yam or Chinese yam, commonly used in traditional Chinese cuisine.
  • 荷插 - A farming tool, often associated with rural life.
  • 被发 - A hairstyle that can symbolize a wild or unrefined state, often associated with hermits or recluses.
  • 黄毛 - Yellow hair, which can be a metaphor for a person with a wild or untamed appearance.
  • 败僧衲 - Tattered Buddhist robes, indicating a poor or neglected state.
  • 岩谷荆莽 - Rocky valleys and dense undergrowth, describing a wild and rugged landscape.

Proper Nouns

  • 仪真报恩长老子照 - The name of the author, a Buddhist monk.
  • 绍兴 - The reign title of Emperor Gaozong of Song.
  • 湖南 - The name of the province in southern China.
  • 淳化 - The reign title of Emperor Taizong of Song.
  • 度牒 - A Buddhist ordination certificate.
  • 山蓣 - A type of yam or Chinese yam.
  • 荷插 - A farming tool.
  • 被发 - A hairstyle.
  • 黄毛 - Yellow hair.
  • 败僧衲 - Tattered Buddhist robes.
  • 岩谷荆莽 - Rocky valleys and dense undergrowth.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 绍兴二十八年 - The 28th year of the Shaoxing era, corresponding to 1158 CE.
  • 外舅 - A respectful term for one's father-in-law.
  • 杨紫微 - A reference to a specific individual, likely a scholar or official.
  • 陈申公俊卿 - Another reference to a specific individual, likely a scholar or official.
  • 小著 - A term for a lower-ranking official or scholar.
  • 省中 - Refers to the government office or administrative center.
  • 国史局堂 - Refers to the hall of the National History Bureau.
  • 大著 - A term for a higher-ranking official or scholar.
  • 雉经 - A form of suicide by hanging.
  • 趣命解之 - Means 'quickly ordered to be freed,' referring to the attempt to save the man.
  • 僭居此位 - Means 'to occupy this position presumptuously.'
  • 殊厚颜也 - Means 'to feel very ashamed.'
  • 除大著 - Means 'appointed as a high-ranking official.'
  • 仓卒 - Means 'in a hurry' or 'suddenly.'
  • 应兆 - Means 'a sign or omen.'
  • 赵汝言宇允之 - Refers to a specific individual, likely a scholar or official.
  • 遗女 - Means 'orphaned daughter.'
  • 浮熙乙未之冬 - Refers to the winter of the year 1155 CE (乙未年).
  • 暴疾 - Means 'sudden illness.'
  • 兄 - Refers to a brother.
  • 怜其孤幼 - Means 'to pity his orphaned youth.'
  • 念之甚至 - Means 'to think of him deeply.'
  • 金紫老人 - Refers to an elderly man wearing gold and purple, likely a high-ranking official or scholar.
  • 凌待制 - Refers to a specific individual, likely a scholar or official.
  • 弹指红尘二十年 - Means 'twenty years in the mortal world, like a snap of the fingers.'
  • 归来瀛海浩无边 - Means 'returning to the vast ocean of the immortals.'
  • 梦魂相遇因随念 - Means 'meeting in dreams because of thoughts.'
  • 珍重前生兄弟缘 - Means 'cherish the brotherly bond from a previous life.'
  • 处世休论大小年 - Means 'in life, do not judge by age.'
  • 瀛关从此断尘缘 - Means 'from now on, the connection to the mortal world is severed.'
  • 芝阶云路逍遥处 - Means 'a place of freedom and ease in the clouds and on the steps of jade.'
  • 羽盖飞鲵不用鞭 - Means 'the carriage of feathers and the flying fish do not need a whip.'
  • 住子已无恙 - Means 'the daughter is safe and sound.'
  • 缘因念结 - Means 'the bond is formed by thoughts.'
  • 暂相遇耳 - Means 'a brief meeting.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 绍兴二十八年 - The 28th year of the Shaoxing era, a period during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 杨紫微 - Likely a reference to a specific scholar or official, possibly a member of the imperial court.
  • 陈申公俊卿 - Likely a reference to a specific scholar or official, possibly a member of the imperial court.
  • 小著 - A lower-ranking official or scholar, often involved in administrative tasks.
  • 省中 - The government office or administrative center where officials worked.
  • 国史局堂 - The hall of the National History Bureau, responsible for compiling historical records.
  • 大著 - A higher-ranking official or scholar, often with significant influence.
  • 雉经 - A form of suicide by hanging, often associated with despair or moral integrity.
  • 趣命解之 - An attempt to save someone who is hanging, reflecting the urgency and concern of the situation.
  • 僭居此位 - Occupying a position presumptuously, suggesting a sense of guilt or shame.
  • 殊厚颜也 - Feeling very ashamed, indicating a strong sense of moral responsibility.
  • 除大著 - Being appointed as a high-ranking official, a significant promotion.
  • 仓卒 - In a hurry or suddenly, suggesting a rushed and unexpected event.
  • 应兆 - A sign or omen, indicating a deeper meaning behind the events.
  • 赵汝言宇允之 - Likely a reference to a specific scholar or official, possibly a member of the imperial court.
  • 遗女 - An orphaned daughter, indicating a tragic personal loss.
  • 浮熙乙未之冬 - The winter of the year 1155 CE, a specific historical context.
  • 暴疾 - Sudden illness, often associated with unexpected and severe health issues.
  • 兄 - A brother, indicating a familial relationship.
  • 怜其孤幼 - Pitying his orphaned youth, showing compassion and care.
  • 念之甚至 - Thinking of him deeply, indicating a strong emotional connection.
  • 金紫老人 - An elderly man wearing gold and purple, likely a high-ranking official or scholar.
  • 凌待制 - A specific individual, likely a scholar or official, whose name is preserved.
  • 弹指红尘二十年 - Twenty years in the mortal world, like a snap of the fingers, reflecting the brevity of life.
  • 归来瀛海浩无边 - Returning to the vast ocean of the immortals, suggesting a spiritual or afterlife journey.
  • 梦魂相遇因随念 - Meeting in dreams because of thoughts, indicating a supernatural or spiritual connection.
  • 珍重前生兄弟缘 - Cherish the brotherly bond from a previous life, suggesting a belief in reincarnation or past lives.
  • 处世休论大小年 - In life, do not judge by age, emphasizing the importance of character over age.
  • 瀛关从此断尘缘 - From now on, the connection to the mortal world is severed, indicating a transition to a higher plane of existence.
  • 芝阶云路逍遥处 - A place of freedom and ease in the clouds and on the steps of jade, suggesting a heavenly or spiritual realm.
  • 羽盖飞鲵不用鞭 - The carriage of feathers and the flying fish do not need a whip, indicating a state of grace and freedom.
  • 住子已无恙 - The daughter is safe and sound, indicating a positive outcome.
  • 缘因念结 - The bond is formed by thoughts, suggesting a deep and meaningful connection.
  • 暂相遇耳 - A brief meeting, indicating a fleeting but significant encounter.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大火 - Literally 'great fire,' used to describe a significant conflagration.
  • 灼烂死者 - Means 'burned to death,' describing severe burns.
  • 神告何其昭昭也 - Means 'the divine warning was so clear,' emphasizing the clarity of the divine message.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 淳熙 - The era name of Emperor Xiaozong of Song (1162–1189).
  • 庚子 - The stem-branch combination used in the Chinese calendar to denote the year.
  • 平江 - A historical name for Suzhou, a major city in present-day Jiangsu Province.
  • 常熟县 - A county in present-day Jiangsu Province, historically known for its agricultural productivity.
  • 许浦 - A place name, likely referring to a military outpost or garrison.
  • 冠服坐殿上 - A figure dressed in official robes sitting on a throne, symbolizing authority and divine or imperial presence.
  • 军令状 - A formal document of responsibility, often used in military contexts to ensure accountability.
  • 郭外塔院 - A pagoda temple located outside the city wall, a common feature of Chinese urban landscapes.

Proper Nouns

  • 淳熙 - The era name of Emperor Xiaozong of Song.
  • 庚子 - The stem-branch combination used in the Chinese calendar to denote the year.
  • 平江 - A historical name for Suzhou, a major city in present-day Jiangsu Province.
  • 常熟县 - A county in present-day Jiangsu Province.
  • 许浦 - A place name, likely referring to a military outpost or garrison.
  • 冬衣 - Winter clothing.
  • 戍将 - The commanding officer of a military unit.
  • 冠服坐殿上 - A figure dressed in official robes sitting on a throne.
  • 军令状 - A formal document of responsibility.
  • 郭外塔院 - A pagoda temple located outside the city wall.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 谶记 - Cryptic sayings or prophecies.
  • 省试 - Provincial examination, a key part of the imperial examination system.
  • 唱名 - Calling out names, referring to the announcement of results.
  • 疫 - Epidemic or infectious disease, a common occurrence in historical contexts.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 平江 - A place name, likely referring to a region in modern-day Jiangsu Province.
  • 唯亭 - A place name, likely referring to a town or village near Pingjiang.
  • 穹隆山 - A mountain name, likely referring to a specific mountain in the region.
  • 震泽 - A place name, likely referring to a town or village near Pingjiang.
  • 岁除夜 - The night before the Lunar New Year, a significant cultural event in Chinese tradition.
  • 国学 - The national school, a prestigious educational institution.
  • 廷试 - The imperial examination, the highest level of the imperial examination system.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄由 - The name of a person who achieved success in the imperial examination.
  • 姚氏 - The name of a family, likely referring to a specific household in the region.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 手寫法華經 - Writing the Lotus Sutra by hand.
  • 每執筆，必先齋素，盥沐更衣 - Every time she held the brush, she would first observe a vegetarian diet, wash, bathe, and change clothes.
  • 以經之力，今即往生烏程縣廳吏蔡家作女 - Due to the power of the scripture, she was reborn in the household of a clerk named Cai in Wucheng County as a girl.
  • 其母夢韻來別 - Her mother dreamed that Yun came to bid farewell.
  • 舟人有女，堯舉調之 - The boatman had a daughter, and Zhaogong teased her.
  • 舟人防閒甚嚴，無由得間 - The boatman guarded his daughter very strictly, there was no way to get close.
  • 既引試，舟人以其重扃棘闱，無它慮也 - After the examination, the boatman had no other concerns because the examination hall was locked.
  • 比兩場皆然，遂與舟女得諧私約 - The same happened in both rounds, so he was able to meet secretly with the boatman's daughter.
  • 觀前欲視其榜，旁一人忽掣去 - The official wanted to see the list, but a person beside him suddenly pulled it away.

  • 秦书人字密附日下，日字左笔下连，而人字左笔斜贯之，踪迹固矣 - Zhou interpreted Qin's writing of 'person' to mean that Qin would remain close to the emperor, with the left stroke of 'person' intersecting the left stroke of 'sun.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 女闻之，窃谓傅姆曰 - The girl heard it and secretly told her wet nurse (傅姆: wet nurse or caretaker).
  • 女子托身惟择所归 - A woman's marriage is only about choosing a good match (托身: entrusting one's body, i.e., marriage).
  • 年之长少貌之美丑，岂论也哉 - Age and appearance, whether young or old, beautiful or ugly, are not matters to be considered (岂论: how can they be considered).
  • 家人颇识女意 - The family began to understand the girl's intentions (颇: somewhat, somewhat deeply).
  • 媒议他姻，则默不乐 - When the matchmaker discussed other marriages, she remained silent and unhappy (默: silently).
  • 父母怪之，曰：“岂宿缘耶？” - Her parents were puzzled and said, 'Could it be a matter of destiny?' (怪: to find strange, puzzled; 宿缘: predestined fate).
  • 乃遣媒通约 - They then sent a matchmaker to make the arrangement (通约: to negotiate the marriage).
  • 陈初固拒以年长非偶 - Chen initially refused, saying that her age was not suitable for marriage (固拒: firmly refused; 非偶: not a suitable match).
  • 其议屡格 - The proposal was repeatedly rejected (屡格: repeatedly blocked).
  • 女辄忧愤或愠不食 - The girl became worried and angry, sometimes refusing to eat (辄: always; 愠: to be angry).
  • 父母忧之，固请，不得已乃委禽焉 - Her parents were worried and insisted, but she could not refuse and finally agreed (委禽: to agree to the marriage proposal).
  • 女喜甚，既成婚，伉俪和鸣，抚陈之二女如己所生 - The girl was very happy. After the marriage, she lived in harmony with her husband and treated Chen's two daughters as if they were her own (伉俪: husband and wife; 和鸣: living in harmony; 抚: to treat as; 如己所生: as if they were her own children).
  • 谓陈曰：“女已长，婚对当及时，不宜缓也。” - She told Chen, 'Your daughter is growing up, and it is time to arrange her marriage; it should not be delayed.' (不宜缓: should not be delayed).
  • 朝夕屡以为言，且广询媒妁 - She repeatedly urged this and also widely consulted matchmakers (朝夕: day and night; 以为言: to urge; 广询: to widely consult).
  • 不半载而嫁其长女，倾资奉之 - Within half a year, she married her eldest daughter and spent generously on the wedding (倾资: to spend all one's resources).
  • 陈曰：“季女尚可二三年。” - Chen said, 'The youngest daughter can wait another two or three years.' (季女: youngest daughter).

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 瑶出，门亦随闭 - 瑶离开，门也随即关闭，表示迅速或意外的离开。
  • 回视殿堂皆神物塑像 - 回头一看，殿堂里都是神物的塑像。
  • 亟趍出门 - 急忙出门。
  • 所乘竹故在 - 所骑的竹制交通工具（可能是竹马或竹车）还在那里。
  • 仓卒复跨之 - 仓促间再次跨上它。
  • 瞑目，觉去愈疾 - 闭上眼睛，感觉走得更快。
  • 如行三里所 - 好像走了三里路。
  • 忽若马蹶，堕地惊顾 - 突然像马一样跌倒，掉在地上，惊慌地四处张望。
  • 乃在城濠侧，已昏暗严鼓后矣 - 发现自己在城濠边，天已经黑了，严鼓已经敲响。
  • 褰衣揭水，攀垝垣以入 - 提起衣服，蹚过水，攀上破墙进入。
  • 至其日，访城东朱氏 - 到了那天，去城东的朱家。
  • 闻其女病甚 - 听说她的女儿病得很重。
  • 瑶固已疑 - 瑶已经怀疑。
  • 徊翔邻近 - 在附近徘徊。
  • 至午后闻其家哭声甚哀 - 到了午后，听到朱家的哭声非常悲痛。
  • 移顷哭声遽止 - 过了一会儿，哭声突然停止。
  • 询之，云女复苏矣 - 询问后，他们说女儿已经苏醒。
  • 瑶怪其事颇验 - 瑶对这件事感到非常惊讶，认为很灵验。
  • 暨复访茅君，则室已虚矣 - 后来又去拜访茅君，但他的房间已经空了。
  • 自是暇日，时一至城东密访其邻 - 从那以后，每逢闲暇，时常悄悄去城东拜访朱家的邻居。
  • 皆云朱氏女自还魂，神识不复如旧 - 都说朱家的女儿自己复活了，但精神意识不再像以前那样。
  • 至不识其父母兄弟，但口时问靳瑶何在 - 甚至不认识自己的父母兄弟，只是经常问靳瑶在哪里。
  • 瑶因托媒氏通意 - 瑶于是委托媒人传达心意。
  • 父母闻瑶姓名已骇愕，遽入谓女曰：“靳瑶今来议汝姻矣。” - 父母听到瑶的名字已经很吃惊，急忙进去对女儿说：“靳瑶今天来讨论你的婚事了。”
  • 女曰：“此我夫也。”自此口不言靳瑶 - 女儿说：“这是我丈夫。”从此不再提靳瑶。
  • 其家竟以归之 - 朱家最终同意将女儿嫁给他。
  • 它日瑶从容访以朱女及其故妻前事，皆懵然不省云 - 有一天，瑶从容地询问朱家女儿及其前妻的事情，但她们都茫然不知。

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 瑶 - 瑶是一个人名，可能是历史或虚构人物。

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 瑶出，门亦随闭，回视殿堂皆神物塑像 - 这一系列动作应视为一个事件：瑶离开，门也随即关闭，回头一看，殿堂里都是神物的塑像。
  • 亟趍出门，所乘竹故在，仓卒复跨之 - 这个句子描述瑶的匆忙离开和他迅速返回骑上竹制交通工具。
  • 瞑目，觉去愈疾，如行三里所，忽若马蹶，堕地惊顾 - 这一系列描述瑶的快速旅行、突然跌倒和惊慌失措。
  • 乃在城濠侧，已昏暗严鼓后矣 - 这个句子表示瑶发现自己在城濠边，天已经黑了，严鼓已经敲响。
  • 褰衣揭水，攀垝垣以入 - 这个句子描述瑶提起衣服，蹚过水，攀上破墙进入某个地方。
  • 至其日，访城东朱氏，闻其女病甚 - 这个句子介绍了瑶去城东朱家的原因，听说朱家的女儿病得很重。
  • 瑶固已疑，徊翔邻近，至午后闻其家哭声甚哀，移顷哭声遽止 - 这一系列描述瑶的怀疑和他对朱家哀悼的观察。
  • 询之，云女复苏矣 - 这个句子报告了朱家关于女儿复活的说法。
  • 瑶怪其事颇验 - 这个句子表达了瑶对这件事的惊讶和认为它很灵验。
  • 暨复访茅君，则室已虚矣 - 这个句子表示瑶后来去拜访茅君，但发现他的房间已经空了。
  • 自是暇日，时一至城东密访其邻 - 这个句子描述了瑶之后的频繁秘密访问朱家。
  • 皆云朱氏女自还魂，神识不复如旧 - 这个句子报告了朱家关于女儿复活和精神状态变化的说法。
  • 至不识其父母兄弟，但口时问靳瑶何在 - 这个句子描述了女儿不认识自己的家人，但经常问靳瑶在哪里。
  • 瑶因托媒氏通意 - 这个句子解释了瑶决定使用媒人传达他的感情。
  • 父母闻瑶姓名已骇愕，遽入谓女曰：“靳瑶今来议汝姻矣。” - 这个句子描述了朱家对瑶名字的反应和他们决定讨论瑶的婚事。
  • 女曰：“此我夫也。”自此口不言靳瑶 - 这个句子报告了女儿认出瑶是她的丈夫，并从此不再提靳瑶。
  • 其家竟以归之 - 这个句子表示朱家最终同意将女儿嫁给他。
  • 它日瑶从容访以朱女及其故妻前事，皆懵然不省云 - 这个句子描述了瑶有一天从容地询问朱家女儿及其前妻的事情，但她们都茫然不知。
